patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH 0/4] nfsd: follow-on fixes for directory delegations

version_history.subject: [PATCH 0/4] nfsd: follow-on fixes for directory delegations

series_items.subject: [PATCH 1/4] nfsd: check for FILEID_INVALID in setup_notify_fhandle
series_items.patch_summary: This patch adds a check for the FILEID_INVALID value in the setup_notify_fhandle function to prevent an out-of-bounds read when encoding the file handle onto the wire. The existing check only caught negative return values, but exportfs_encode_inode_fh can also return this invalid value if the buffer is too small. By adding a check for this specific value, the patch ensures that the file handle is encoded correctly and safely.

series_items.subject: [PATCH 2/4] nfsd: use empty string for directory name in NOTIFY4_CHANGE_DIR_ATTRS
series_items.patch_summary: This patch fixes a security vulnerability in the NFSv4 server by ensuring that directory names are not leaked to clients when sending NOTIFY4_CHANGE_DIR_ATTRS events. The code was previously using the actual directory name, which could cause cache confusion and potentially expose local namespace information. To fix this, an empty string is now used for the directory name, as specified in RFC 8881 Section 10.4.3.

series_items.subject: [PATCH 3/4] nfsd: check delegation status in nfsd4_cb_notify_done
series_items.patch_summary: This patch fixes a bug in the nfsd kernel module where an NFSv4 callback function would spin indefinitely if a delegation had been closed or revoked, preventing the delegation from being freed. The fix involves adding a check at the top of the nfsd4_cb_notify_done function to terminate the callback promptly when the delegation is no longer valid. This ensures that the sc_count reference is released and the delegation can be freed as expected.

series_items.subject: [PATCH 4/4] nfsd: fix ino_t format specifier in nfsd_handle_dir_event tracepoint
series_items.patch_summary: The patch fixes a format specifier issue in the nfsd_handle_dir_event tracepoint, updating it to use u64 for inode->i_ino. This change is necessary due to inode->i_ino being upgraded to a u64.

review_comments.author: Chuck Lever
review_comments.summary: Fixed the issue in place and pushed an update to nfsd-testing, making this patch obsolete.
review_comments.raw_body:
On Sun, May 24, 2026, at 8:09 AM, Jeff Layton wrote:
> On Sat, 2026-05-23 at 12:17 -0400, Jeff Layton wrote:
>> inode->i_ino is now a u64. Use the same size for the tracepoint.
>> 
>> Signed-off-by: Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>
>> ---
>>  fs/nfsd/trace.h | 4 ++--
>>  1 file changed, 2 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)
>> 
>> diff --git a/fs/nfsd/trace.h b/fs/nfsd/trace.h
>> index 3d0f0bd30d90..1d5d11f914f2 100644
>> --- a/fs/nfsd/trace.h
>> +++ b/fs/nfsd/trace.h
>> @@ -1384,7 +1384,7 @@ TRACE_EVENT(nfsd_handle_dir_event,
>>  	TP_STRUCT__entry(
>>  		__field(u32, mask)
>>  		__field(dev_t, s_dev)
>> -		__field(ino_t, i_ino)
>> +		__field(ino_t, u64)
>
> My apologies. This should read:
>
>                 __field(u64, i_ino)
>
> I had the fix in my tree and forgot to commit it before sending. Would
> you like me to resend this one, or do you just want to fix it up?
>
>>  		__string_len(name, name ? name->name : NULL,
>>  				   name ? name->len : 0)
>>  	),

I fixed this in place and pushed out the update to nfsd-testing.


-- 
Chuck Lever

patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch series addresses several issues related to directory delegations in the NFS server (nfsd). The patches fix problems flagged during review, including checking for FILEID_INVALID in setup_notify_fhandle and using an empty string for the directory name in NOTIFY4_CHANGE_DIR_ATTRS. Additionally, the patches check delegation status in nfsd4_cb_notify_done and correct a format specifier issue in the nfsd_handle_dir_event tracepoint.

review_comments.author: Chuck Lever
review_comments.summary: Applied the patches to nfsd-testing without any issues. No further comments or concerns were raised.
review_comments.raw_body:
From: Chuck Lever <chuck.lever@oracle.com>

On Sat, 23 May 2026 12:17:33 -0400, Jeff Layton wrote:
> Just a few small fixes for problems that Sashiko noticed flagged during
> review. None are super-critical but it'd be good to have them fixed
> before the feature ships.

Applied to nfsd-testing, thanks!

[1/4] nfsd: check for FILEID_INVALID in setup_notify_fhandle
      commit: fb1d813dcb193d52322e3320543c029c2931f613
[2/4] nfsd: use empty string for directory name in NOTIFY4_CHANGE_DIR_ATTRS
      commit: 9e8c221e056f6bf4a525fa5d74ecad735855935d
[3/4] nfsd: check delegation status in nfsd4_cb_notify_done
      commit: 62fdbd7c4ce24aee5aa9698c256047d2e4071f30
[4/4] nfsd: fix ino_t format specifier in nfsd_handle_dir_event tracepoint
      commit: 04cc256388df5d2d284f4d9d3e9f22ebf974f806

--
Chuck Lever <chuck.lever@oracle.com>

review_comments.author: Jeff Layton
review_comments.summary: Responded to Chuck's application of the patches by apologizing for a mistake in one of the patches and offering to resend it or have it fixed up.
review_comments.raw_body:
On Sat, 2026-05-23 at 12:17 -0400, Jeff Layton wrote:
> inode->i_ino is now a u64. Use the same size for the tracepoint.
> 
> Signed-off-by: Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>
> ---
>  fs/nfsd/trace.h | 4 ++--
>  1 file changed, 2 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)
> 
> diff --git a/fs/nfsd/trace.h b/fs/nfsd/trace.h
> index 3d0f0bd30d90..1d5d11f914f2 100644
> --- a/fs/nfsd/trace.h
> +++ b/fs/nfsd/trace.h
> @@ -1384,7 +1384,7 @@ TRACE_EVENT(nfsd_handle_dir_event,
>  	TP_STRUCT__entry(
>  		__field(u32, mask)
>  		__field(dev_t, s_dev)
> -		__field(ino_t, i_ino)
> +		__field(ino_t, u64)

My apologies. This should read:

                __field(u64, i_ino)

I had the fix in my tree and forgot to commit it before sending. Would
you like me to resend this one, or do you just want to fix it up?

>  		__string_len(name, name ? name->name : NULL,
>  				   name ? name->len : 0)
>  	),
> @@ -1394,7 +1394,7 @@ TRACE_EVENT(nfsd_handle_dir_event,
>  		__entry->i_ino = dir ? dir->i_ino : 0;
>  		__assign_str(name);
>  	),
> -	TP_printk("inode=0x%x:0x%x:0x%lx mask=%s name=%s",
> +	TP_printk("inode=0x%x:0x%x:0x%llx mask=%s name=%s",
>  			MAJOR(__entry->s_dev), MINOR(__entry->s_dev),
>  			__entry->i_ino, show_fsnotify_mask(__entry->mask),
>  			__get_str(name))

-- 
Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>
